content:
Every night, same bus stop.

A 72 year old father waits there, not because he has nowhere else to be, but because his daughter finishes work late and he refuses to let her walk home alone in the dark.

No security guard. No cab service. Just a father who decided her safety is his job, every single night, at this age.

We spend so much time talking about ambition, growth, hustle. But somewhere, a man in his 70s is teaching us what real commitment looks like. Not a Linkedin post. Not a TED talk. Just showing up. Daily. Without being asked.

This is what protection actually looks like. Not grand gestures. Just presence.

Tag someone whose father would do the same.

Follow Maya Rajbanshi for insights
comment: The overlooked part is how much invisible care work happens outside formal systems. Families often become the safety net, but communities and workplaces also need to design for safer commutes.
